channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 271 replies — 1084.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 271 • Updated: 2026-07-11 19:23 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62466875
>>62466579

62466937
>>62466875
>everything depends on GME bulls
>the GABBBYQ KEKS need fantasty RP for their theory

62467452
Hello friends. Are we tuning in to Jake's space call today? Should be lots of fun!

62467493
>>62467452
Thank you for the reminder, King! I almost forgot!

62467912
Jake2SNEED is the goat.

62468829
>>62467912
He is very based. No other stock community has anyone close to Jake. He is the bbbyq champion.

62468834
Nothing happens until the divorce is finalised and GABBBYQ has controlling interests on the board

GME bulls are in charge now

62469811
The cusip-6 is in charge

62470216
I voted YES for the Cusip Conundrum

62470348
TLDR from the spaces call with Jake2Sneed?

62470385
>>62470348
RC took control of BBBYQ using HBC as a proxy. They were selling RC the "diluted shares" and he took control of the company. I guess there were some inferences to the revelation that RC is an invested party to the CH11 bankruptcy which caused some definitional changes to wording to exclude "affiliate". He is the only candidate that matches up with this change. So effectively we now know that RC is in control of the bankruptcy and we're probably in the clear to see the CH11 wrap up at any given moment. We're past all of the required waiting periods and we're just waiting to wagmi at this point. It could happen as early as Monday, but we'll probably find out between now and October that we're all millionaires.

62470436
>up to 1k views the jerk2b youtube
>67 unique IPs in the thread with only than a third dabbing
it appears that this thread's continued undead existence is not due a single autist with proxies, but there's an actual cult with dozens of baggies
I hope humanity will evolve from this kind of behavior eventually

62470477
>>62470385
Still need to wait on DNA results from post divorce filings Alex, do not be hasty.

62470489
>>62470348
We won

62470525
>>62470489
We won so hard

>Those who $GABBBY cream it

62470558
>>62470385
I still don't understand how RC would be able to get that many shares without reporting it publicly. Isn't the threshold 5%? Also, whatever happened with the B. Riley deal? Did those shares go to a mystery benefactor like we speculate with HBC, or am I forgetting something? I would really like for him to fucking do something with this already.

62470584
>>62470558
The reporting requirements for financial institutions are different than when they are held by individual investors. There was a longer duration for him to have to file. The difference between the forms is like, he has to report in 5 or 10 days as himself, but 30 days because an entity was buying them. I can't remember exactly what the reasoning was but ABC covered this in his space two weeks ago. They didn't realize what was happening until it was too late. They thought they were getting death-spiraled but in reality it was RC doing himself a lil.. uh... you know... hehe... slurpy wurpy ;)

62470592
>>62470558
He was prosecuted for failing to report and took a plea bargain

62470732
>>62470592
>retard shilltranny doesn't know the difference between civil and criminal court
uh yeah, bathbros, i think we won

62470781
>>62470558
>no documents of Ryan owning said shell company
>no documents saying Ryan owns the shadow holdings
>all done in backroom at the holiday inn handshake deals
>only $GABBBYQ has her inner thighs inked “RC was here” as proof of part ownership in PP Ventures

62470793
>>62470732
>gets sued by the federal government
>but it's a civil case not a criminal one!
like it matters

62470808
>>62470793
not sure how it works in India but in America plea deals aren't a thing in civil court

62470811
>>62470808
He settled the lawsuit by paying a criminal fine to one charge and the government dropped the criminal fines to the other two charges.

62470822
>>62470811
yeah idk what youre trying to prove but it wasn't criminal court so it wasn't a plea deal. try again next week

also how very interesting bath bros. bonds just changed their name to match DK-Butterfly

62470841
>>62470822
>yeah idk what youre trying to prove
I'm saying he was prosecuted and took a plea agreement. The federal government sued him and fined him and he agreed that he had broken the rule so he paid the fine.

that's how he got away with not reporting. Anon asked how he got away with it. He got caught and paid the fine.

62470846
>>62470822
paying a million dollar fine on a 68 million dollar profit is a perfectly acceptable business expense. Sure, he broke the law, but he still made 67 million dollars off you guys.

62470854
>>62470846
and when you realize he took $68,000,000 from BBBY baggies, you understand that HE didn't pay that million dollar fine for his rulebreaking

you guys did. It came out of the money he fleeced you for.

62470858
>>62470854
But I guess before anyone can admit that RC took all their money they'd first have to admit that their shares are worthless

and nobody itt has moved to that stage of grief yet
despite having 3 plus years to get there

62470875
>>62470385
>>62470489
>>62470525
>>62470781
Quite the reverse UNO if the board was rubbing their hands together as they death spiraled the Gabuss right into RCs bedroom.

62470884
>doesnt know the difference between civil and criminal court
>doesnt know you can put 4 posts into 1 post
Bangalore education sure is something

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 154 replies — 616.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 154 • Updated: 2026-07-11 19:24 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62461781
I just realized I'm going to need to learn how to pay taxes.

62462300
>>62461781
https://www.irs.gov/individuals/free-tax-return-preparation-for-qualifying-taxpayers if you make less than 69k a year you probably qualify

62462365
trucking. self-driving trucks are a meme and still decades away, if you're a company driver you can pull down 80k clean with little overhead.

most companies will hire you on the spot if you have a CDL and a clean record

62462409
>>62459478
yeah you arre going to have to drive miles to your job
you DO have a car right op?

62462413
>>62462365
>if you have a CDL
they have pivoted to only hiring jeets as of a year or two ago

62462416
>>62460802
it's all jeets and other browns that cheat the system by having 3 drivers share the same car and uber account
you're lucky to make minimum wage after gas, tax and wear and tear

62462448
>>62462416
Actually, sharing a car makes sense. You at least split the cost of buying a car between you. And the insurance.

62463196
>>62462448
Triple the maintenance, but you have support from your other drivers

62464516
Is playing the lottery viable?

62465774
Sorry, I need the thread to stay up a little longer.

62465843
>>62449961
Similar to you OP, 40 years old, only 1 year office job experience.
Although I did learn programming in my free time. I am also taking care of me and my mother's estate which includes 3 rental properties and a sizable stock portfolio. I also taught myself ancient Greek, some Japanese, and some Russian. I read about business and finance daily.
I'm pretty sure I could be useful in an office setting, especially one finance or real estate related. Not necessarily because of my real estate or finance knowledge, but by being very comfortable using unix utilities, setting up servers, writing programs and scripts, and setting up web services. That, in addition to understanding, tax, finance, and renting.

I just don't know how to frame all these for an employer. I'm thinking it'd be easier to start an online agency.

62466261
>>62465843
You sound like you've got it made compared to me. Good luck on your agency.

62466338
>>62449961
Close your eyes.
Imagine a career.
Now make it into a resume.
There is no employment gap.

62468866
And, bump

62468886
Work should be vocational. Just do voluntary stuff that you feel is important or do your own thing. Anything else makes one miserable.

62468898
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2f4qnU1oOoc

62468936
>>62449961
I'm in a similarish position to this guy. I'm 38 and I'm thinking of becoming an actuary. I'm a math PhD dropout (have a master's I guess but I never bothered to apply for graduation) and I've basically been NEETing since. Work part time in a warehouse but that's not relevant to anything. Now someone my age switching to actuary isn't unheard of but usually they have a continuous record of experience in something that can give them an advantage, like being a teacher or w/e. I haven't taught since grad school which was a decade ago. I have nothing to show for my gap. Unironically how fucked am I? I have no chance of being hired recent grads because of my gap that only possibly be interpreted as laziness and apathy right? Passing the first round of exams would be easy but it costs money and it's likely for nothing if I don't get hired.

62469039
>>62449961
Felling like sharing my story with you OP:

I've dropped out of college in 2016 because I learned too little too late that the degree I was aiming was worthless.

After some shitty jobs, I moved in another country to be a traditional gunsmith (old lath, files,eating steeldust daily, basically turning raw material to toys for adult) but I wasn't very good at boot licking my teachers.I did acquire all the skills I need but the last thing they do to fuck me in the ass was to denied me the diploma for shady reasons.

Went back to my hometown, did a lot of different jobs (from manufacturing to car insurance assistant) while trying to figure out a way to be a gunsmith and I did found one.

Moved out from my dad's home (so he could not drink 1/4 of my paycheck anymore) for a city 200km away, I was happy since I could finally trying to live a normie life testing guns, even for a pay way below what I was providing to the company (+140€ every two month for risking getting shrapnel'd alone in a soundless booth) and it lasted 8 months before they found the level of lead in my bloodstream was too high (apparently air evacuation was failing and they didn't feel the need to inform me) and I was fired for "incapacity to do the job".

Moved back with my mother to get healthy again and I did, but apparently my resume scare the normies, even when I try to lie or sweeten it, so since I live in a touristic area with old farts waiting for the sweet embrace of the reaper and turbowelfares negros of all colors, I tried to get into another trade but the joboffice providing refused 9 of my business plans so I went basic and started by buying a van to sell drinks.

The van was a scam and I'm at the edge of being a welfare-wigger.

I'm 30, I hate my communist shithole of a country (France), I had more than 40 different jobs and I don't even know what to do anymore beside learning trading and workout.

Just don't give up.

62469070
>>62468866
With respect kill yourself.
Your excuse for not applying to jobs is "it's 4am fuckface" but you keep waking up at fucking 6pm and going to sleep at 8am.
You're not helping yourself at all stop pretending and stop bumping this fake engagement thread (blog).

62469080
just say your a nigger or poopjeet

62469085
I am 39 and haven’t had a job in over 10 years..

62469146
nigger just do what foids do get a comfy wfh hr, middle manager, project manager, customer liason, customer service or other phone operator job.

62469410
We shouldn't have to go to some corporation just to get an income.

62469809
>>62449961
Not fucked at all. Just lie on your resume, they lie about the job description, it's all bullshit. I was a NEET for 10 years after high school and now I'm on track to retire at 50. You really only need 20 years of working to make it, if you're smart with your money.

62470572
>>62457986
>>62457969
neither answered because they made that shit up lol

62470630
>>62449961
Literally just lie. Lie, lie, and lie some more. Accept that you can only live a lie because no one normal will accept and understand your situation. Stay disciplined and keep chipping away at things daily until you find an opportunity. There's a Chinese proverb that "Heaven never seals off all exits".

62470681
>>62449961
>almost 40
>NEET
>not 7 figures yet, probably not even in 6 figure hell
Enjoy being part of the permanent underclass

62470736
You have two options, just lie on your resume, or apply to a job where they take anyone. The latter is typically telemarketers and collections agencies and shit like that. If you lie on your resume you will need someone who can pose as references for you. Reach out to friends and family to fake references. You may have to get a dedicated phone number for them.

62470784
>>62470736
>>62458848
I'm also lying on my resume, this is the thread I made 62470742. How feasible is the lie I made?

62470870
>>62451269
man, giving up "for a while" is like 6 months of not working, maybe a year. If you couldnt get a job, any job, in 20 years and hold it down, you are below nigger tier. 60 IQ crackhead noghs can manage to show up to their job at Wendy's often enough to not get fired

BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 145 • Updated: 2026-07-11 18:47 UTC

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

62449396

62449400

62449441

americans are not people.

62462287

62462621

62462950

I've seen people abuse their return program, and get their membership canceled.

62462960

i would cover her face with semen if you catch my drift

62463582
>>62449441
BENCHOOOOOD!!!!!!! WHY YOU REDEEM!! NO NO DO NOT CLICK ON THAT!!!! NOOOOOOOOOOO!!!!!!

62463635
>>62458606
cucks here won't do shit, they bitch and complain while patronizing Indian establishments. They will talk about the take over and then also about their favorite totally awesome pajeet they met at signhortons. The most opposition you will get is from white canadians. People will whine about the state of tim hortons while simultaneously pretending a coffee from there is a mandatory purchase they will die without

Sad, but they will have to watch canada just disintegrate with no option to fix it before they maybe realize. This whole country is full of just chicken shit low IQ cattle. Maybe 4% of canadians realize how bad this actually is and are doing the bare minimum like not hiring and giving money to the invaders, the rest are fully, entirely complicit in it. If it wasn't for how big this place was, that you can find thousands of acres with not a single person within 5km of you, I would have already left.

62464135
>>62463582
Saaars

62464324
>>62463635
Canada is just India now accept it

62464711
>>62462960
Kek

62464984
>>62449441
kek'd at the replies

62464997
>>62449441
I fucking hate Indians so much man

62465006
>>62449846
lol. Source?

62465010
>>62449441
easy (you)s

62465617
>>62465010
Cope

62465950
>>62449388
+1000 izzat

62466351
saaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaar

62467929
ssaaaaaaaaars!

62468640
>>62465950

62468814
>>62449388
went yesterday, some pajeeta was loading a plastic bag with ice from the soda machine

62469260
waiting for the dump

62469664
>>62468814
kek

62469708
>>62449388
4 day old thread

62469713
I live in a mostly white small boomer city and I see whities return the dumbest things like TVs after superbowl, potted plants after winter, and opened food. People are nasty.

62470795
>>62469713
kek

item.title: Oil markets are predicting doom — 16 replies — 64.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Oil markets are predicting doom

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-07-11 19:23 UTC

OP

I've been thinking about this a lot lately. The price changes in the oil market make basically zero sense unless you factor in massive demand destruction from a global economic recession.

Let me walk you through it. First, let's compare the price changes in the wake of the Ukraine war to the price changes after the Iran war. As you can see, the price shock from the Ukraine war was actually worse and lasted longer, despite the fact that Russia supplies less than half the oil that flows through the strait of Hormuz.

Now, I know what you're thinking, right, this is because the MOU was signed which ended the war, right? But the MOU never actually got implemented. Almost none of the oil that normally flows through the strait actually got out. The only reason prices didn't get worse was because massive amounts of reserves were released from pretty much every country around the world who keeps reserves.

Here's the thing that doesn't make sense though, if the MOU was never implemented and countries have been drawing down their reserves all this time, it means that rationally speaking those reserves should be about to run out in the next couple of months and prices would be expected to spike. Traders should be anticipating this and pricing in the higher price already, but they didn't even move from the announcement that the MOU was over and the war was back on.

The only thing that explains this is that this is what traders think the price will be without a return to normal from shipping in the strait. The only way that can happen when the strategic oil reserves run out is if there is massive demand destruction from economic recession.

Early replies (first 3)

62470570
>>62470561
ya pretty much

62470571
>>62470561
Also if you look at the graph, you can see that there is a downward trend in oil prices starting october of 23. This suggests that demand destruction was already happening since then and the general trend would have continued without the spike from the Iran war. If you extrapollate the downward line on that trend, you can see the current prices are significantly higher than where it would have been, thus the strait closure is actually being priced in.

62470573
I expect jewish tricks as always to supress oil prices.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62470581
the strait can be opened, all we have to do is give iran everything they want including undisputed control of the strait. trump can do this at any time and profit, no one wants to price anything in too much when a scam like that is possible

62470583
>>62470573
My guess is that it's mostly coming from China right now because their economic downturn is significantly worse than they are letting on. We all know they constantly cook the books and on paper China never has a bad day according to the government, but in reality I expect this is pretty much as bad as 08 was for them.

62470595
>>62470561
>force majeure stands in your path
“heh. nothing personal, kid”

62470602
>>62470583
China is about to bring out the money bazzoka. I think they’re just waiting for the fed to blink

62470606
>>62470561
There's no system wide demand destruction, just look at the 3-2-1 cracks. The reality is that the market is running on headlines and speculators are massively shorting crude.

62470613
>>62470606
this
traders are just being retarded / greedy
oil will spike up soon

62470617
>>62470606
Bait. OP effort posted about the trend, not the last 3 months. The trend has gotten back in line and moving down

62470622
>>62470606
Cracks are blown out because the great military minds have been blowing up refineries all over the world

62470738
>effort post of a looming recession gets ignored on /biz/
This board isn’t smart anymore

62470800
>>62470617
The driving point of his post is that Hormuz is still closed and oil prices are down so he concluded that demand destruction has happened. What he didn't take into account:
>product demand which is the true indicator of global recession
>at the moment there's enough crude because of commercial inventories, strategic reserves and a mini glut out of Hormuz
>crude is getting shorted by speculators and is almost net-long negative while commercial users have little say in it currently
>>62470622
The utilisation rates of non destroyed ones indicate that the world still wants its products, i.e. little to none demand destruction happened.

62470803
>>62470800
this
the OP is actually retarded
he's saying "oil futures price is down therefore demand will go down as well cuz reasons" lol
literally nothing about the current situation indicates demand is going down

62470810
But anon, there are no traders anymore. It's just algos now.

62470886
>>62470738
Has this board ever been smart?

item.title: Everest MiCA CASP — 19 replies — 50.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Everest MiCA CASP

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-07-11 19:07 UTC

OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

Early replies (first 3)

62461998
>>62461988
I thought it would have mooned harder from the news. What gives

62462006
It is because we are meant to suffer as token holders just like the Linkies

62463131
>Unlisted from coingecko
Lmao!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62463754
It’s still on coingecko so you need to learn how to use google search correctly my friend

62464735
>>62461988
nobody is buying your bags bro and this MICA shit will not pump your very heavy bags lmaoooo

62465284
>>62461988
The flaw tih Everest is that you're trying to pump a project that supports regulations preventing P&D, with a "utility" token that has zero use outside being used as gas in beta test by one or two b2b customer, no fabled VNO until now as well, so where's the utility?

It is clear that the MICA application was not because it is mandatory, but it is also a last ditch attempt to make themselves more attractive to raise external funds, because there's no way they could obtain meaningful capital by selling more token at this price, so they get MICA to pitch it to institutional while it still warm; before inevitably many other similar project also obtain MICA and overtake Everest. Reminder that Everest has lost grounds on KYC, fiat on ramp, RWA products even to companies that don't use proprietary token.

62465296
>got it before Binance

oof

62465554
>>62465284
>It is clear that the MICA application was not because it is mandatory
Yeah anything you say can’t be taken seriously if you typed that shit

62465690
Everest > Binance

ID is priced into identity verification, eKYC/AML checks and payments, with a portion of each fee burned. Licensed exchanges lack identity stacks; identity protocols lack MiCA authorisation. Everest combines both: a demand structure most tokens lack.

62466022
How many tokens to make it?

62466677
>>62466022
0

62468922
0 as in G0ysl0p maxxer

62469223
>>62461988
Just bought a phat stack. Don’t let me down mate

62469237
Unless this is Cousin talking, your fat stack is also a big fat liar kek

62469711
>>62461988
There are no two coins more cucked than link and everest

62469862
ID and Link love OTC, that is why both will never have any real movement or volume in real life

62470282
>>62461988
The 1c wall has been breached bullflag forming for 10c breakout

62470465
1 cent will be resistance next few years, break out 2030

62470842
>>62461988
Kek you holders are still alive? Chainlink tonight roped because of this

item.title: The Graph (GRT) is finally shutting down. — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The Graph (GRT) is finally shutting down.

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-07-11 19:27 UTC

OP

The Graph (GRT) is finally shutting down. They pausing the grant program. That means no more dev grants or growth expected.

Early replies (first 3)

62469674
>>62469640
it's over

62469700
Based on our forecasting model, Chainlink is expected to exhaust its subsidy program by approximately 2030. At that point, the ecosystem enters a transition period in which operational expenses are no longer sufficiently offset by protocol-generated fees. Assuming current trends continue, the remaining treasury reserves are projected to be depleted by roughly 2033.

The model assumes CCIP generates only around $100 per day in revenue (current revenue model), equivalent to approximately $36,500 annually. Against a hypothetical operating budget measured in the tens of millions of dollars per year, this represents a negligible contribution toward sustaining development, infrastructure, and staffing costs.

In this scenario, subsidies cease in 2030, forcing operations to rely almost entirely on fee revenue and the accumulated reserve.

Assume that from the beginning of 2026 through the end of 2029, the organization accumulates reserves at a rate of $1 million per week. (52 weeks × $1 million = $52 million per year ; Four years (2026-2029) = approximately $208 million in accumulated reserves by the start of 2030.)

Now assume the protocol generates only $100 per day in fees: $100 × 365 = **$36,500 per year

Against an assumed operating budget of $52 million annually (equivalent to paying 260 HR employees earning $200,000 per year, that revenue offsets only about 0.07% of yearly expenses, leaving an annual shortfall of roughly $51.96 million.

Under those assumptions, the reserve of approximately $208 million would disappear surprisingly quickly:

Year 1: $208M - $156M
Year 2: $156M - $104M
Year 3: $104M - $52M
Year 4: $52M - effectively exhausted

In other words, a persistent annual burn of around $52 million, combined with negligible fee generation, would consume nearly the entire reserve in about 4 years. Any additional expenditures (expansion, infrastructure growth, bonuses, or hypothetical executive compensation packages) would shorten that runway even further.

62469732
>>62469700
Full circle. They fund the core business with token sales and exit into a financial services company with perpetual prior funding from token holder cultists who never realized the business did not reference their token whatsoever. It is actually insane how transparent it is and how passionately some people still fall for it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62469748
>>62469640
WTF, I lost thousands in this shit. Fuck the anons who shilled this shit.

62469784
>>62469700
>>62469732
notice how chainlink posts just show up like this?

62469913
did more digging. look here. their P/F is 3,700x. This means their project is 3,700x underwater. deeply flawed economic structure is only capturing 4k cash flow per month. How the fuck are the nodes surviving on the 3% annual inflation?

62469917
>>62469913
don't the nodes just receive tokens that they then sell?

62469934
>>62469917
yes, the 3% inflation hard coded into the protocol. Here is the rest. This shit is wild. The $4K on the chart simply proves that the token itself is failing to capture the economic value of the network.

https://tokenterminal.com/explorer/projects/the-graph/financial-statement

62469938
I'm actually baffled. The software is a roaring success, but the cash flow model for token holders is currently broken which is exactly why they are panicking and pausing the grant program to fix it. This is why GRT never goes up. They broke it on purpose? For the holders? This shit needs to be investigated. Were they stealing the holder rewards for themselves?

62470033
Unironically lost thousands on this.

62470039
>>62469640
well at least im not the only one who lost everything

62470896
THE GRAPH IS KEK'S CHOSEN COIN
$9.99 EOY

item.title: VOO and Chill — 222 replies — 81.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 222 • Updated: 2026-07-11 16:46 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62459187
>>62403053
The median 64 yo is planning to work until they're dead.
Boomers are never going to retire because they can't afford to. They are going to keep working like the good little goyim they are and fall down dead on the job because that's the only way they can fund the monthly payments on all the toys they went into debt to purchase.
I spit on their graves. Fuck boomer scum.

62459238
>>62459187
Lmao, another white incel who knows everything,,,,,what are the odds? 100% at least,,,,,do they use it to get rich? Lmao no

62460695
Yawwwwwn another week another VOO purchase

62460733
>>62460695
i made a whole $5 buying a share of VOO during that dip :) too bad I lost more from all my previous VOO shares

62460835
>>62460733
Sorry to hear that champ maybe DRAM is more your speed

62460844
>>62400995
Lmfao. You do realize that the highest weighted stocks that lead the spy are mostly in QQQ right? Lmfao

62460852
>>62459187
They can afford it

62461238
>>62401051
>I think a decade long CAGR comparison was a 5% difference but I’m not sure if CAGR is the best stat to go by?
It changes significantly depending on when you enter the market. Since 2000 there's only a 0.6% difference in favor of QQQ over SPY, the latter outperformed the former for most of that timeframe. If you bought sometime post-2009, however, you would have been better off holding QQQ by far. The moral of the story is keep an eye on things and only buy more volatile assets if it's a growth period. QQQ will continue to be great if you bought a decade ago until the music stops.

62461412
>>62400515
Fpbp. Bogleheads are the biggest retards on the planet.

62461672
>>62400620
>What makes one rich
Health, family, friends.

62462476
>>62461238
For now I’m still just doing VOO cause it just feels better even if I’m missing out on qqq gains

62464487
It’s payday tomorrow, VOO brahs. There’s nothing risky that looks too appealing so I go all voo maybe some palantir as well. My only regret is taking the work health benefits, my check will be smaller. Two weeks is an eternity to wait for your money.

62464830
Is there any point of voo and chill if I already have a 401k and pension. Know can compound it more but can be more risky right

62466388
>>62400493
buy lottery tickets

62467200
>>62466388
But VOO, QQQM if you want more return and volatility

62467206
>>62460835
no no i don't buy dogshit. thanks

62467215
>>62464830
retire earlier
imagine if you have enough VOO in your brokerage that you can afford to live off its dividends only. you'll pay virtually no tax and enjoy 10% growth per year indefinitely.

62467367
>>62467206
I was being sarcastic I’m a Boglehead

62468767
>>62461672
Am i rich if i have health, family, and friends but i'm stuck wageslaving 50 hours a week and perpetually stressed and taking orders like a bitch and doing endless donkey work just to watch my bosses and clients get more money and freedom than me?

62468775
>>62400493
Buying voo only makes sense if you have size.

62468793
>>62468775
Is 7”x5” enough?

62468838
Reckless strategy. Practically gambling. Do you even ACWI IMI?

62468882
>>62468767
The great majority of people need to work to live. And there are many miserable "rich" people and many happy "poor" people. You will come to learn.

62468885
How much are you guys DCA'ing into the shit and piss 500? I'm doing about $2,500 every monthy and I'm at a little over $780k.

62468889
>>62468885
Idk 12% of my paycheck for however long it's been I don't look at it

62468891
>>62468885
Also, I have noticed that the people complaining about the returns from the S&P500 are people who don't have a job and only have a lump sum to invest.

62468920
i got 100k saved up from wageslaving, how do invest it to chill and earn with etf's?

also should i buy these etf directly from vanguard?

62468957
>>62400636
This was me at 35 just doing the bare minimum to match at my company. It wasn’t 150 but 280 but you get the idea.

I’m now 39 and maxing out my HSA/Roth IRA/Roth 403b/bonds. It’s close to 60k a year I’m putting way and I still have 20 years to catch up.

It can be done but so many of my peers are barely saving and living above there means it’s crazy.

62470092
>>62468920
Just put it into VOO
If you mean literally how do you invest just download any brokerage app
If you want more risk do QQQM

62470503
>>62461412
T. Poorfag

Open thread • Replies: 109 • Updated: 2026-07-11 18:03 UTC

OP

Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Early replies (first 3)

62466535
They should just make corporate espionage legal in western countries at this point. The only people who are winning from the current laws are the Chinese

62466548
>>62466527
>state media says
lol

62466549
>>62466546
Show me one innovation from Japan.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62468346
Glad I didn’t buy space x stock

62468390
>>62468335
Fair points, but whenever possible, I try to deal with people in good faith (its a white trait, or flaw, depending on the situation)
The only way out of a low trust civilization is to deal with others in good faith, and violently expelling them permanently if they step out of line. Sadly, we can't do the latter half of that anymore and so here we are

62468428
>>62468297
>>62468335
What? Sorry I couldn't hear you I think you're breaking out? KRRSSHRHSHH KRRSHH...hat? Sorry could yoKRRRSCHH KRRSCH...peat that?

62468437
Holding a bag of spcx $210

62468446
>>62468297
Totally agree. I just assume all other posters are Indian as a default at this point. Mods complain about spam but won't do the one thing that would kill spam forever. Flags and range bans.

62468568
>>62468297
Nigga, your national debt is owned by Asian and Middle Eastern shitholes. Shut your bitch mulatto mouth.

62468589
>>62468568
>Nigga
>your national debt

ESL talking like a nigger. Another reason we need flags.

62468596
>>62468335
I lived in the USA for most of a decade and I hate you just as much

62468632
>>62468596
I don't remember asking, but thanks for your input. I'll add it to the list of reasons we need flags.

62468768
>>62466527
He boghted the spacesex ponzi ? kek

62468783
>>62466527
SPCX sisters?

62468833
>>62468568
25% is foreign owned in the form of bonds. Read a book

62468851
>>62466548
you know all the big news agencies of your country are state media right? It's just more covert

62468864
>>62466905
>They're only good at copying
Americans will say this while replying on a Chinese manufactured phone, then have an oolong tea, hit reply, put the phone down, and then eat a Chinese Boy Piss Egg with chopsticks and say we don't make anything new.

62468867
>>62468851
Such as? Name them, cunt.

62468872
>>62468864
Are you ok?

62468987
>>62466549
anime girls

62469293
>>62466527
cuz elon hires chinks and poos who will bring back or sell the tech to china

62469347
>>62466610
It's a very interesting story, but a part that is easy to forget is that he had an investor fully bankrolling his efforts for multiple years with $3 millions.

62469665
this is some shit that a university student should be able to do as a minimum requirement. wtf is so complicated?

62469774
>>62468335
>Although /sp/'s average IQ level is (maybe?) below this board's average,
Seriously?? How many people here bought Chainlink or Safemoon? Not even mentioning all the Indian shitcoin scams.

62469777
>>62469347
>a part that is easy to forget is that he had an investor fully bankrolling his efforts for multiple years with $3 millions.
So what? How is that even relevant to the point that it was a Japanese scientist who invented the blue LED?

And the "investor" was the Japanese shit-tier semiconductor company he worked for, whose Japanese CEO saw the business deteriorating and threw the money at the Japanese scientist as a "hail Mary" attempt to create a product that would save the Japanese company.

"OMG he had an investor!!!" So fucking what?

62469825
>>62469777
>So fucking what?
None of this would happen without the $3 million investment. None of this. The guy was extremely lucky in this regard that he had such an opportunity.

62469856
it's using a net lmao

62469886
>>62466527
Did China steal blueprints from SpaceX?

62470354
>>62469886
Long March 10B is about as different from Falcon 9 as a rocket can get while still being a rocket that does propulsive landing. Cable catch, dual fuel, seven engined booster, closed cycle engines, oxidizer-rich preburner, etc.

62470360
>>62466535
Corporate espionage happens all the time regardless of its legality and there's zero chance that the Chinks aren't just being used by Western alphabet agencies. The moment they come up with, test, build or create anything that is a legitimate threat you can expect everyone involved to suicide by two shots to the back of the head or for the entire complex to have a rocket accident and get blown up in the explosion. People do not share power, especially not over international borders.

>>62466548
Also this. You can generally assume that everything China ever says is just lies projected to a face-saving culture. Its for domestic consumption, not for us to speculate about.

62470370
Buy SPCX, the prices won’t get much lower than this. You don’t want to be an earth bagholder, do you?

62470418
>>62470360
>The moment they come up with, test, build or create anything that is a legitimate threat you can expect everyone involved to suicide by two shots to the back of the head or for the entire complex to have a rocket accident and get blown up in the explosion

They should have done that before Chinese satellites ruined Trump's Iran adventure by providing Iran with the exact locations in real time of air defenses, aircraft parking spots and other assets in the region, as well as providing damage assessment so Iran didn't waste missiles on an already destroyed target

62470662
>>62466603
>The one really great thing about this is that maybe now China will stop crashing boosters onto their own citizens' villages.
It's due to the fact their spaceport is so far inland it's impossible to not drop on it's citizens. Russia's fall over Siberia, while USA shoots over oceans.

item.title: >shitty particle board bedroom dresser is disintegrating >look into buying something nice, made of hardwood >$3-5k avera… — 70 replies — 73.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
>shitty particle board bedroom dresser is disintegrating >look into buying something nice, made of hardwood >$3-5k avera…

Open thread • Replies: 70 • Updated: 2026-07-11 18:33 UTC

OP

>shitty particle board bedroom dresser is disintegrating
>look into buying something nice, made of hardwood
>$3-5k average price for a single bedroom dresser (not a set)
is this economic progress? a SINGLE ITEM of hardwood bedroom furniture, something that was commonplace 50 years ago, is now a luxury good, to the extent that it costs like 10% of the annual post-tax wages of the average american worker?

Early replies (first 3)

62466672
Fiat debases the value of work even compared to wood

62466682
>>62466672
it's truly insane. i'm not looking for like something made of continuous slabs of old growth redwood. $3k is basically the price floor on something made of commodity grade oak or whatever.

62466688
>>62466682
You are paying for the labor costs.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62467803
>>62467770
Cheap garbage practical furniture always existed anon, only the nice hardwood stuff gets preserved.

62467814
>>62466823
you sleep on the floor and just now realize you are poor??? lmao

62467904
>>62466665
How often are you looking to rebuy the dressers
Like once every 2-3 years?
Or rather going for that yearly refresh?

62467916
>>62467065
It doesn't work like in Ferngully where the heckin forests are being logged for wood.
99% of Western wood demand is served by planted tree farms.
(Note: this is only about the civilized world)

62467937
>>62466823
My most expensive piece of furniture right now is my futon that was made in the country I live.
It's a thin Japanese style one so I basically sleep in the floor too.

62467954
>>62467916
Yeah because the deforestation already happened in the west. Retard

62468034
>>62466665
I bought a house recently and wanted to get a dresser that would last me so I chose real hardwood. It's high quality and heavy, but the fucking faggot makers of the dresser give you cucked, short rails that only allow the drawers to open halfway. I guess one retard opened the drawers all the way and it tipped and now we all have to suffer? Either way, returning it would of been too much of a hassle so I replaced the drawer rails and now the drawers open fully. Of course none of the retards reviewing it mentioned how the drawers don't slide out all the way.

62468057
>>62466823
Welcome in the low status male club, chud.

62468164
>>62466823
For me it's a little different. It makes me realize that I'm downwardly mobile. My parents had nice furniture when we were kids and still have nice furniture. They were able to afford a house and a few kids after ~5 years of marriage.

I'm in my mid-30's, can't afford a house or nice furniture. I keep telling myself things will get better but my student loan is like a chain ball locked to my leg.

Not sure how I'm going to escape lower-middle class life, it'll actually take a miracle.

62468192
Should I pick up making custom cabinetry as a side hustle?

62468302
>>62468192
Its not a side hustle, despite being a box with a door the hard part is fitting them into not-exactly-square rooms. If you half ass it, it will look and perform like an indian did it

62469259
>>62466665
what you do instead is buy 4,000 dollars worth of shop equipment and make your own furniture like men in the past did.

62469287
>>62467803
>only the nice hardwood stuff gets preserved.
Hardwood is a botanical term, there's lots of wood which while it's 'hardwood' is softer and shittier than softwood.

62469290
>>62467954
Actually the US is more heavily forested than 150 years ago.

62469299
>>62468164
>Not sure how I'm going to escape lower-middle class life, it'll actually take a miracle.
Have you attempted grabbing your bootstraps and lifting?

62469301
>>62468302
can't that be done with small screw-adjustable standoffs and some filler material? does it really take more than that?

62469303
>>62466665
i have particle board stuff thats lasted a long time and looks nice. makes me wonder what that one boomer was ranting about all those years ago about particle board sucking when mine has lasted for a long time, a decade plus

62469309
>>62466665
hardwood is not for poors.
this is economic progress.
brown ids get out of this thread.

62469312
>>62469303
Particle board only lasts 20-30 years usually. Hardwood you only have to buy once or get it for free from dead relatives. Seriously The wood will grow back by the time someone needs to replace it. By the way there is a middle ground of interlocked boards instead of one wide piece.

62469321
>>62468164
im retardmaxxing, what other choice do i have? slavery into my 60s/70s? no fucking thanks. glad im not in europe where so many nations are unanimously installing suspiciously similar laws, ie pic related or moving forward with deporting ukrainian male refugees back to ukraine

62469326
>>62469299
Now that's good advice

62469342
>>62466665
Facebook marketplace

62469383
>>62466665
It used to be a lot more than that comparatively
But if you want some quality hardwood dressers look on craigslist or other apps people use to get rid of stuff.
There are plenty of people trying to get rid of stuff but its going to be real heavy

62469927
>>62468034
i noticed this too. we suffer so that retards may live.

62469929
>>62466665
How much does a tree sapling cost? How much wood does a tree yield in 5 years? Should we all be lumber farming?

62469978
>>62467904
this, buy once cry once.

62470119
>>62469929
$150
not enough to make one dresser lol
you don’t own the kind of land you would need to to grow wood for profit

62470650
I assume someone else has already made the point but nice hardwood furniture is frequently given away or sold very cheaply on places like craigslist and marketplaces. Age only makes them nicer as long as they get a refresh every once in a while

62470655
>>62466752
>a set of furniture
Bro! Never buy sets. It looks cheap and tacky. Watch 30 mins of random interior decoration videos and you'll understand

62470745
>>62466665
all the forests have been cut down for farmland already so inexpensive wood era is over and marketcaptured regulations and taxes wont allow the entrance of new tree farming.

item.title: Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news? — 93 replies — 19.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news?

Open thread • Replies: 93 • Updated: 2026-07-11 14:50 UTC

OP

Seems like every BTC pump or dump is blamed on ETF flows or macro headlines.

>Is that just the reality now, or are people overlooking other factors?

Early replies (first 3)

62450753
>>62450728
Its 100% cz dependent, just tradingview has to write something to the price action.

62450760
>>62450728
Btc does this thing where it lulls the newfag into a false sense of superiority. Both in bull and bear markets. In a bull market, it tricks newfags into thinking it will go up forever and traps them in overleveraged longs and in bear markets, it tricks them into it never going back up and they either sell low, sell covered calls or lever up short and get milked. Just hold spot and don't pay attention for a decade.

62450902
>>62450753
cz?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62463265
>>62450728
ETF’s = whales

62463279
>>62459225
You are forgetting every nations currency have the strength of wet noodles now. They will print again soon.

62463285
bitcoin is a commodity backed ETF without the commodity

all it ever is and all it ever will be

62463301
There should be a 90s themed live action series called something cheesy like "Good morning Mr. Nakamoto" about the bitcoin creator. After making it big in his mid 20s he has become a multi billionaire, but is extremely paranoid so he decided to keep a low profile. Now he's in his late 30s, a family man with a couple kids, and the series follow an episodic format where he desperately tries to hide from his family his enormous wealth and just pretends to be an office worker. The thing is he has zero credentials so he can't actually get a job so it becomes incrementally difficult (due to plot inducement stupidity) to maintain the farce. Prying yet endearing secondary characters keep coming and leaving, and oftentimes misunderstandings make him almost blow his cover. During some weaker seasons he's stalked by some group of nerds that are almost sure he's the real deal and want to take revenge due to prices plunging, but eventually some shenanigan makes the nerds pin their suspicions on Nakamoto's rival (who was trying to cuck him with his wife, who by the way goes through a very annoying arc because she realizes his hubby is not being open with her or something). Nakamoto's personality is a bit like Hal from Malcolm in the middle but less lolrandom, he just plays as a fool to better hide his secret.

62463320
>>62463301
Some thematic episodes could include his wife hiring a financial advisor because he played the broke ass bit too much, and he tries to stop the advisor from peeking at his 100b net worth at any cost. He also gets in trouble because the advisor is not very bright and tells terrible advice very overconfidently, so Nakamoto counters with suspiciously good and sharp analysis (fortunately his wife just doesnt care because he has ingrained the dumb with money narrative and the advisor is very confident, and also a financial advisor duh). In another episode he gets pinned by the IRS after accidentally doing some billion level transaction and having to pay tenths of millions in taxes. Another one where he (again, Plot) is revealed to have all the money behind a 20 word seed, but he doesnt remember the words, so it's a musical where he finally manages to recall them through a catchy song. The episode is rated quite poorly though because it doesn't fit with the general tone. In the seventh and last season his younger kid finds out by dumb luck and the rest of that season is him preventing them from telling mom. The show gets cancelled afterwards due do decline in viewership and the plot never gets finished.

62463429
>>62463301
>>62463320
This has potential

62464033
>>62463263
but it doesn't mean it'll stay that way.

62464098
>>62463429
I could foresee an actual ending where he has to let all his earnings go because of some contrived dumb reason, but it's framed in a way that looks somewhat deep and poignant. His cover is blown in the process, but as he's revealed to be the creator of Bitcoin, he suddently gets a call from one of the companies he tried to apply the most though all the seasons (let's say it's a mid-sized company that offers an average salary, it has it's wacky employees and boss, they usually appear for like 10 minutes every few episodes just to reject Nakamoto). He's finally accepted and he merrily joins (it's a subtle joke because now that he's a public figure he could easily be gunning for 500k jobs, but he settles for this as a nudge to the audience), managing to achieve the life he pretended to have (and actually wanted) from the get go. The series end with him walking into the office's slightly rundown and shabby building with a bright smile. You even get to make that cheap overused allegory of ''choosing happiness and being true to your principles over riches''. I feel like there's an alternate universe where this would have killed it.

62464115
>>62463256
I'll keep testing TBVs on Aave V4. BTC has a lot more going on now than just being a store of value.

62464121
>>62463301
The best ending would be nobody ever finding out if it's really him. Keeping the mystery alive is probably the most Satoshi thing possible.

62464142
>>62464098
You're too invested in that drama. It's not happening in real life.

62464251
>>62464121
You know what? That's fair. I relied too much on 90s sitcom tropes. It should just end episodically, there's just no more seasons and that's it.
>>62464142
I don't have much going on in my life and my family and friends find my occurrences boring. And it's supposed to be a comedy. What drama? I want it to be goofy and for all publics!

62464760
>>62464033
Take stablecoins for another example. Zero. Zilch. Nada. $0.00 -- Remember when 100% of USDT used to be on Bitcoin? Now it's 0%, Omni is dead and Tether has moved to every other chain. There is nothing more to Bitcoin than BTC the asset.

62464820
I have a credit card that gives me cash back in the form of BTC. So my investment strategy is just skimming off my purchases anyway.

62465005
>>62464251
Why do you think you're right about that and you think you're so sure about it?

62465089
>>62465005
I don't understand the question. Right about what.

62466102
>>62464115
Cool. There's clearly more happening around bitcoin now than there was a few years ago.

62466109
>>62464760
I'm talking about native BTC, not stablecoins. Those are two different discussions.

62466120
Imagine having to pay for each website you visited…that’s how stupid it is for proof of work, btc will never work long term (already sucks now)

62466195
>>62459656
Well this is a link board so I won't say anything negative. I hope link goes to 10k eoy for all the anons holding it.

62466244
>>62466102
You do realize Aave is on Ethereum, right? It's great for BTC the asset but not so great for Bitcoin the network. When you're saying innovation isn't happening on Bitcoin base layer but "around it", do you mean on Aave for example?
>>62466109
And I was talking about innovation on Bitcoin in general. Originally I replied to
>why are people still building new infrastructure around it?
saying there's no infrastructure, well... besides bridging BTC to Ethereum. Saying there's no stablecoin infra on top of Bitcoin is relevant to the discussion.

62466717
>>62466244
Bridging is risky

62466757
>>62466195
Chinlink

62467179
>>62466120
What drugs you're taking ser?

62467263
>>62466195
buy link

62467380
>>62463301

62468734
>>62463320
I remember this .how time flies

62469658
>Icp

62469661
>>62464820

62470315
>>62469658
I don't know any reason to sell

item.title: Sell your crapto and pivot to AI, we still have plenty of room to grow: 1. — 28 replies — 19.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sell your crapto and pivot to AI, we still have plenty of room to grow: 1.

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-07-11 18:03 UTC

OP

Sell your crapto and pivot to AI, we still have plenty of room to grow:

1. Nebius Group (NBIS) — Hyper-growth Cloud Leader
Q1 2026 revenue: +684% YoY.
2026 projections: ~544–550% revenue growth; 2027 ~225–234%.

2. Sandisk (SNDK) — Memory/Storage Explosive Gainer
YTD stock gains: ~465–857% (top performer in many trackers).

3. Micron Technology (MU) — Memory Powerhouse
YTD gains: ~300–1000%+ in some periods; one-year leader in many AI indices.

4. Broadcom (AVGO): AI semiconductor revenue +143% recently, with Q3 guidance >200% growth. Large-scale with strong margins.

5. Seagate (STX), Western Digital (WDC): Storage plays with 180–550%+ gains.


There's no catalyst for crapto and won't ever be and we both know it.

Early replies (first 3)

62469845
No ma'am, I cannot accept sexual favors as compensation because that's not what we agreed on with the terms. Perhaps we can work out a payment plan? Can you please put your pants back on?

62469846
>>62469805
You want to make money? Make games like pic related

62469852
>>62469846
Saturated. There are villages in my home country that are devoted to making these now. Everyone contributes different aspects to the game.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62469858
>>62469805
I also consider following silicon-makers and related (acc. to ai)
what u guys think?
AMD
STX - seagate - magnetic (and ssd) disks gone up, as SSD/mmem dries up
TSM - tsmc - obvious
MU micron

AMAT applied materials - silicon supply
LRC - Lam Research - same
TKY - Tokyo Electron Ltd - same
CNN1 - Cannon Inc. - same (as part of their offer)
NIB - Nidec Corm - same as Cannon

how about also some MORE makers of tools needed to make silicon/litography?

how about also some related to raw materials? copper? others?

how about power realated companies? and uran realted?

other ideas?

62469865
>sell the crypto bottom for the ai top

lmao

62469870
>>62469852
What's the best one?

62469872
>>62469865
/biz/ makes sense when you realize this is just where the Greater Fools hang out

62469880
>>62469805
Are you just listing what already pumped, saying now it's a good time to buy it?

62469887
>>62469865
Many such cases

62469892
>>62469872
>>62469887

>pay massive taxes
>move the funds into a tradfi stock trading account
>buy the top of AI stocks with half the money you had in crypto because u lost half to taxes
>AI stocks dump 90%
>crypto starts pumping again

62470054
>>62469805

62470060

62470091

62470111

62470121

62470218

yes.

62470245

62470271

nya nya nyat thEreS LitERLLy a SaUcE WATerMaRK yOu ReTarD nya nya

you dont pay taxes on losses frend

62470301

62470322

62470392
Crypto is never going up again. There are no more retards to be conned into buying it. It does nothing. It has no use case.

62470396
>>62469805

62470402

62470491

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2 — 85 replies — 14.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

Open thread • Replies: 85 • Updated: 2026-07-11 13:36 UTC

OP

Brothers,

We spent 20 days and 203 posts together in the last thread and we have yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen. We knew this wouldn’t be easy so let's not get dispassionate.

For my next proposal I will say we focus on developing a skill that will increase our value. If we all level up on our own it will only take longer. If we all level up by 2-3 points a month then cumulatively we will all have gotten stronger as a mass society. Perhaps one amongst us can elevate themselves and come back to teach the others!

How long shall we be waiting then? How long will our own potential be mocked by others? To what limit will we continue to persist in the underclass?

Come, those who have not $10,000 to their name

Let us commence the general!

>Good /biz/ manga
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Good /biz/ movies
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Born_Rich_(2003_film)

>Good /biz/ books
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Gambler_(novel)

>Good /biz/ games
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Good /biz/ waifus
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_Holmes

>Agenda 2030 is on the way, have you studied what your enemy has planned out?
https://www.math.uwaterloo.ca/~ervrscay/TheGreatReset.pdf

Praeviously
>>62373752

Early replies (first 3)

62453729
All you faggots need to do is buy chainlink. Fuck your side hustles. You can get a suicide stack for 8k right now.

62453795
Last thread we spoke a lot on learning new skills but very few fruitful things arose. I realized the issue is that a lot of the proposed skills were just boring or too niche.

62453799
>>62453795
wait, are you asking poor people to discuss amongst themselves how to get rich?
kek

you don't learn how to get rich from poor people

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62462992
>UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

OH NO NO NO

AHAHAHAHAHAHA

LMAOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

62464145
>>62462813
Rent truly is the worst bloodsucker. What are you saving for? Just to have reserve funds? You were spending all your excess money on debt and not saving/investing? Genuinely asking

62464163
>>62462813
Also I would be careful with poor nutrition depending on your age. If under 25 then take the risk with cheap foods.

62465232
Bump, almost done waging

62465510
alright today, lets practice looking at good designs of top runners and seeing if we can emulate the artists ability. Let's also look at great /biz/ memes and ask ourselves, ""what made this meme so popular" like it was an actual gallery

62465526
Who is this? What makes him so special? Why is he one of the most identifiable mascots on this site?

62465529
Forgot the image, what about this characters DESIGN made him so well loved

62465547
this is what knowyourmeme has to say on Mumu

side note: alt girls get paid to make videos like this? what a job!

62465548
what will happen when we reach #10k thread?

62465553
the absolute state of this board

62465556
>>62453622
Quads and I’ll chop my cock off

62465561
>>62465556
dude...

62465736
How did this character become the most recognizable image on /biz/?

62465765
and here is information on bobo

62465874
I realize I have lost some anons attentions, let us have a group activity. Let's all draw a variation of Bobo or Mumu. I will go ahead and start!

62465886
>>62465874
make sure you use a Bobo or Mumu as reference!

62466308
>>62465547
>alt girls get paid to make videos like this?
she's been making videos about memes for around 2 years, I guess she just got a deal with KYM now. I want to have sex with her btw

62466618
Fell asleep, will continue this tomorrow

62467689
>grandpa was a millionaire when he died in the 90s
>fucked up his will, his children squandered what was left of it
>everything, to the last cent, gone in two generations
>I get the shitpie of go fuck yourself

I hope the pod is cozy

62467752
>>62467689

62468039
>>62453622

62469068
finally, off work

62469071
>>62453622
lmao holy shit biz is fucked

62469114
>>62469071
here is my picture of mumu!
what do you guys think?

How much money could this net me? Could it

get me a job?
be a nft worth thousands?
make it into a popular meme?

what could I have done better, where was I lacking? feel free to post your own Bobo or Mumu, and we can all see where we need to improve. even we are good at drawing, we don't know where to market it. We might be good at marketing, but poor at drawing. Perhaps there's something my Mumu lacks that is holding it back from being something that others would want to save(remember this, if they save your art, they must like it somewhat)

62469339
here is a picture of my Bobo! Didn't take too long, anyone here could pull it off, feel free to show me yours, come on guys!

That being said there is something missing from my work

62469468
going to bed guys, worked pretty hard, tomorrow I will tell you the missing link

62470058
>>62453622
>Mfw 6 figures and will use these threads to go to 7

62470065
>>62453622
I know two languages, I'm considering doing language classes on italki for pennies. I know AI has fucked up making money out of language but I think there's still a bit of squeezing room from people who would rather talk to an actual person that has an organic grasp on the target language. What do you think? I am running out of ideas.
>>62458801
Powerwash 100 driveways for $100 each 10 times.

62470191
love your attitude op so i've come to bring you knowledge that yall may already know. i'm almost at the 6 figure table.

1. live with your parents, you losers are probably already doing this. with minimal living expenses you can invest more. also allows you to drop the emergency fund which again lets you invest more.

2. be young, youth is an advantage for compounding interest. a huge advantage.

3. get a job, yes a wage slave. obviously a better job will allow you to invest more. it won't be forever. the sooner you hit the rpg grind irl the sooner you can spend the rest of your life eating hot pockets and playing vidia.

4. invest in growth etf's. your crypto is fools gold. yes the stock market can crash but it always bounces back even if it takes years to do it. and you all are young enough to ride out the bad years. once you get old, or have enough then sell your growth etf and get dividend etfs and live the good life.

5. you can't just invest in safe etf's but you have to place them in the right location.

5a) 401k up to employer match, this is the best because its free money for you but the investments are usually not the best. if you have the option get the ROTH 401k not the normal one if the company offers it. for a normal 401k what you put in is tax differed, when you draw from it at 59 1/2 then you pay taxes on it.

5b) ROTH ira is you own personal retirement account. you pay taxes on what you put in but no taxes on what grows inside or when you pull your money out. 59 1/2 also. has a limit on how much you can invest into it each year so max it.

5c) health savings account, hsa. what you contribute can be deducted from your taxes, what grows in it not taxed and when you spend it on a medical expense its not taxed. if you save your bills you can reimburse yourself at a later date once the account grows huge. at 65 you can just withdraw from the account as if it was income.

im just some old fag trying to help my younger brothers.
